trade.summary: The Stati Uniti is Giappone's largest export market and second-largest trading partner overall. Giappone runs a persistent trade surplus with the Stati Uniti, which has historically been a source of friction. Major Giapponeese exports include automobiles (Toyota, Honda, Subaru), electronics, and machinery. Stati Uniti exports to Giappone include aircraft, agricultural products (beef, soybeans, wheat), and LNG. Both are members of CPTPP (Stati Uniti withdrew in 2017 but relations remain strong). A 2019 Stati Uniti-Giappone Trade Agreement reduced some tariffs.
disputes: persistent Stati Uniti trade deficit with Giappone in automobiles historically contentious
Stati Uniti pressure on Giappone to open agricultural markets
Stati Uniti concerns about Giapponeese currency management

diplomatic.summary: The Stati Uniti-Giappone alliance is the cornerstone of the Asia-Pacific security order and one of the most important bilateral relationships in the world. Formalized by the 1951 Treaty of Mutual Cooperation and Security, the alliance includes ~54,000 Stati Uniti troops stationed in Giappone. The relationship transformed from post-WWII occupation into a genuine strategic partnership. Giappone's 2022 National Security Strategy represents a historic shift, doubling defense spending to 2% of GDP and acquiring counterstrike capabilities, all in close coordination with Washington.
recent_incidents: 2023 Camp David trilateral (Stati Uniti-Giappone-Corea del Sud)
Giappone's 2022 NSS — historic defense expansion with Stati Uniti backing
ongoing negotiations over host nation support costs

military.summary: The Stati Uniti-Giappone security alliance is one of the world's most capable. The Stati Uniti stations 54,000 troops in Giappone, operating bases in Okinawa, Yokosuka, Misawa, and elsewhere. Giappone and the Stati Uniti conduct extensive joint exercises, share intelligence through Five Eyes-adjacent mechanisms, and are integrating their command structures. Giappone's acquisition of Tomahawk cruise missiles from the Stati Uniti represents the deepest yet integration of offensive capabilities. The alliance is explicitly designed to deter Cina and North Corea del Sud.
military.alliance_status: Treaty allies — Treaty of Mutual Cooperation and Security (1951, revised 1960)
incidents: Okinawa basing issues remain politically sensitive in Japan
US base crimes create periodic domestic political friction

financial.summary: Giappone is the largest foreign holder of Stati Uniti Treasury securities (~$1.1 trillion), making the two countries deeply financially intertwined. Giapponeese FDI in the Stati Uniti is among the world's largest, spanning automotive manufacturing (Toyota, Honda plants across the South and Midwest), electronics, finance, and real estate. Stati Uniti firms are among the largest foreign investors in Giappone. The bilateral investment relationship is the world's most extensive.

energy.summary: The Stati Uniti has become Giappone's largest LNG supplier following Russia sanctions and deliberate diversification. Stati Uniti LNG exports (from Gulf Coast terminals) to Giappone have grown dramatically. Stati Uniti oil also flows to Giappone. The energy relationship has deepened alongside the security alliance as Giappone deliberately reduced dependence on Middle Eastern and Russian energy.

scientific.summary: The Stati Uniti-Giappone science and technology relationship is among the most productive in the world. Joint research spans semiconductor technology, life sciences, space exploration (JAXA-NASA cooperation on Artemis lunar program), AI, quantum computing, and climate science. Technology transfer is extensive. Both countries co-chair major scientific initiatives and share classified research on defense technologies.
